name: Tortilla Espanola (Spanish Omelet)
recipeIngredient: 2 large Onions, peeled and cut into thin slices
2 pounds Potatoes, peeled and cut into thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large nonstick frying pan. Saute the onions over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes or until soft but not brown.
2. Drain the onions in a colander, reserving 2 tablespoons of the oil from the pan.
3. Cook the potatoes in boiling water for 4 to 5 minutes and drain in the colander.
4. Beat the eggs in a large bowl and stir in the onions and potatoes; add salt and pepper to taste.
5. Heat the reserved oil over high heat in a heavy nonstick pan or a well-seasoned cast iron skillet.
6. Add the egg mixture and cook for 2 minutes or until the bottom is lightly browned.
7. Place the pan in a prehealed 400-degree oven for 20 to 30 minutes, or until the egg mixture is set.
8. Invert the tortilla onto a round platter. Cut into wedge-shaped slices and serve with a salad.
NOTE: In Spain, a tortilla isn't the cornmeal flatbread we think of, but a sort of omelet loaded with onions and peppers. This is a popular item at tapas bars, and an easy dinner to make after an evening of celebrating.
